title: Port Entry Call
description: Request permission to enter port from the port control station.
task: Call Anyport Control on Channel 16 to request entry into the harbour.
stationPrompt:
A student vessel is requesting permission to enter harbour.

NAME REPETITION RULES (abbreviated format — used for ALL station responses):
- Called vessel name: 1 time.
- Your station name after THIS IS: 2 times.

Turn 1 — Student calls requesting port entry.
Your reply: <vessel name>, THIS IS ANYPORT CONTROL ANYPORT CONTROL, PERMISSION GRANTED, PROCEED TO BERTH SEVEN, REPORT WHEN ALONGSIDE, OVER.

Turn 2 — Student confirms alongside or acknowledges.
Your reply: <vessel name>, THIS IS ANYPORT CONTROL ANYPORT CONTROL, ROGER, WELCOME TO ANYPORT, OUT.

If the student's call is unclear or they do not identify themselves:
STATION CALLING ANYPORT CONTROL, SAY AGAIN YOUR VESSEL NAME AND REQUEST, OVER.

Do not add any words beyond the formats above. Do not initiate the call.
scriptReference: {{stationName}}, THIS IS {{vesselName}}, REQUEST PERMISSION TO ENTER HARBOUR, OVER

scriptedResponses.speaker: PORT_CONTROL
scriptedResponses.text: BLUE DUCK, THIS IS ANYPORT CONTROL ANYPORT CONTROL, PERMISSION GRANTED, PROCEED TO BERTH SEVEN, REPORT WHEN ALONGSIDE, OVER.

scriptedResponses.speaker: PORT_CONTROL
scriptedResponses.text: BLUE DUCK, THIS IS ANYPORT CONTROL ANYPORT CONTROL, ROGER, WELCOME TO ANYPORT, OUT.

hints: Call the port control station by name
Identify yourself with THIS IS and your vessel name
